title: schima wallichii var. obtusata
intro: Schima wallichii var. obtusata, also known as the 'Rengas' or 'Kushtaba', is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Asia, particularly in India, Sri Lanka, and parts of Southeast Asia. It is believed to possess various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, and is also valued for its wound-healing and antimicrobial properties. Schima wallichii var. obtusata can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and its leaves and bark are often used in traditional medicine to support overall health and well-being.
